title: Pre-Filter Needs Cleaning
shortDescription: The washable pre-filter needs cleaning to restore airflow.
fullDescription: The A3 indicator on Philips air purifiers means the coarse pre-filter has accumulated enough dust and debris to require cleaning. The pre-filter captures large particles like hair and dust, protecting the HEPA filter behind it.

causes.cause: Pre-filter not cleaned after previous alert
Filter timer not reset after cleaning

fixSteps.title: Remove the pre-filter
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug the purifier. Open the filter cover and slide out the pre-filter (the outermost mesh filter).

fixSteps.title: Clean the pre-filter
fixSteps.instruction: Vacuum the pre-filter surface or rinse under running water. If washing, let it air dry completely (24 hours) before reinstalling.

fixSteps.title: Reinstall the pre-filter
fixSteps.instruction: Once dry, slide the pre-filter back into its slot and close the filter cover.

fixSteps.title: Reset the filter timer
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the filter reset button for 3 seconds until the A3 indicator turns off. On app-connected models, reset through the Clean Home+ app.

whenToCallPro: Not needed. Pre-filter cleaning is always DIY.
videoSearchQuery: Philips air purifier A3 pre-filter cleaning reset

faq.q: How often should I clean the pre-filter?
faq.a: Monthly, or more often in dusty environments.

faq.q: Is the pre-filter washable?
faq.a: Yes, the pre-filter can be vacuumed or rinsed under water. Let it dry completely before reinstalling.

faq.q: Does the pre-filter need replacement?
faq.a: The pre-filter is designed to last the life of the purifier with regular cleaning. It does not need replacement.

title: Activated Carbon Filter Needs Replacement
shortDescription: The activated carbon filter has reached its usage limit and needs replacement.
fullDescription: The C7 indicator means the activated carbon filter (which removes gases, odors, and VOCs) has been in use beyond its recommended lifespan and needs replacement for effective gas and odor filtration.

causes.cause: Filter has been in use for 12+ months
High VOC or odor environment saturating filter faster
Filter timer not reset after replacement

fixSteps.title: Purchase replacement filter
fixSteps.instruction: Order the correct activated carbon filter for your Philips model. Different models use different filter types.

fixSteps.title: Replace the filter
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug the purifier. Open the filter panel, remove the old carbon filter, and install the new one following the arrows for correct orientation.

fixSteps.title: Reset the filter timer
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the filter reset button for 3 seconds. The C7 indicator should turn off.

fixSteps.title: Dispose of old filter properly
fixSteps.instruction: The old carbon filter can be disposed of in household waste. It is not recyclable.

videoSearchQuery: Philips air purifier C7 carbon filter replacement reset

faq.q: How long does the carbon filter last?
faq.a: Approximately 12 months under normal conditions.

faq.q: Can I wash the carbon filter?
faq.a: No, activated carbon filters cannot be washed. They must be replaced.

faq.q: What does the carbon filter remove?
faq.a: Gases, VOCs, odors, formaldehyde, and other harmful gaseous pollutants.

title: HEPA Filter Needs Replacement
shortDescription: The HEPA filter has reached its usage limit and needs replacement.
fullDescription: The HEPA filter alert indicates the main particulate filter has been in use beyond its recommended lifespan. The HEPA filter captures 99.97% of particles as small as 0.3 microns, including dust, pollen, bacteria, and some viruses.

causes.cause: Filter has been in use for 12-24 months
Smoking or cooking accelerating filter degradation

fixSteps.title: Purchase replacement filter
fixSteps.instruction: Order the correct NanoProtect HEPA filter for your model.

fixSteps.title: Replace the filter
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug. Open the filter panel, remove the old HEPA filter (note orientation), and install the new one.

fixSteps.title: Reset the filter timer
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the appropriate reset button. On app-connected models, reset through the Clean Home+ app.

fixSteps.title: Run on turbo briefly
fixSteps.instruction: Run the purifier on turbo/max speed for 10 minutes after installing a new filter to flush any loose fibers.

videoSearchQuery: Philips air purifier HEPA filter replacement NanoProtect reset

faq.q: How long does a Philips HEPA filter last?
faq.a: 12-24 months depending on usage and air quality. The machine tracks usage automatically.

faq.q: Can I wash the HEPA filter?
faq.a: No, NanoProtect HEPA filters cannot be washed. They must be replaced.

faq.q: What is NanoProtect?
faq.a: NanoProtect is Philips' proprietary HEPA filter technology that captures particles as small as 0.003 microns.

title: Air Quality Sensor Error
shortDescription: The air quality sensor is malfunctioning or needs cleaning.
fullDescription: E2 indicates the PM2.5 or gas sensor is not reading correctly. The sensor lens may be dirty, or the sensor element needs recalibration.

causes.cause: Sensor exposed to strong chemicals or aerosols
Sensor needs recalibration after power outage

symptoms: Air quality readings stuck or showing dashes

fixSteps.title: Clean the sensor inlet
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the sensor opening (usually on the back or side). Use a dry cotton swab and compressed air to clean the sensor area.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle to recalibrate
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 60 seconds. Upon restarting, the sensor will self-calibrate over 30-60 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Run in clean air
fixSteps.instruction: Place the purifier in a well-ventilated area with clean air for 1 hour to allow proper sensor calibration.

fixSteps.title: Avoid aerosols near the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Do not spray aerosols, use incense, or apply strong cleaning chemicals near the sensor inlet.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor does not recover after cleaning and calibration.
videoSearchQuery: Philips air purifier E2 sensor error fix cleaning calibration

faq.q: What sensors does the Philips purifier have?
faq.a: Most models have PM2.5 particle sensors and some include gas/VOC sensors and humidity sensors.

faq.q: Can cooking fumes damage the sensor?
faq.a: Prolonged exposure to heavy cooking fumes, smoke, or aerosols can temporarily overwhelm the sensor.

faq.q: How long does sensor calibration take?
faq.a: Approximately 30-60 minutes in clean air conditions.

title: Fan Motor Error
shortDescription: The fan motor is not operating at the expected speed.
fullDescription: E3 indicates the fan motor cannot maintain the requested speed. This can be caused by an obstruction, motor overheating, or a motor driver fault.

causes.cause: Motor overheated from continuous max operation

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug. Look into the air intake and outlet for any objects that could block the fan.

fixSteps.title: Let the motor cool
fixSteps.instruction: If the purifier has been running on max for extended periods, unplug for 30 minutes to cool.

fixSteps.title: Restart
fixSteps.instruction: Plug back in and turn on. If the error clears, it was a thermal protection event.

fixSteps.title: Contact support
fixSteps.instruction: If E3 persists, the motor may need professional service.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after cooling and checking for obstructions.
videoSearchQuery: Philips air purifier E3 fan motor error fix

faq.q: Can running on max damage the motor?
faq.a: Extended max operation can cause thermal protection to activate. Use auto mode for everyday use.

faq.q: How long do Philips purifier motors last?
faq.a: Motor life is typically 5-10+ years under normal use conditions.

faq.q: Is E3 covered under warranty?
faq.a: Motor faults within the warranty period are typically covered.

title: Wi-Fi Connectivity Issue
shortDescription: The purifier cannot connect to Wi-Fi or has lost connection to the Clean Home+ app.
fullDescription: The Wi-Fi connectivity error indicates the purifier cannot connect to your home network. The purifier requires 2.4GHz Wi-Fi and must be within range of the router.

causes.cause: Wi-Fi password changed or network down
Purifier too far from the router

symptoms: Cannot control from Clean Home+ app

fixSteps.title: Check Wi-Fi network
fixSteps.instruction: Verify your 2.4GHz Wi-Fi is working. Philips purifiers do not support 5GHz.

fixSteps.title: Move closer to router
fixSteps.instruction: If the purifier is far from the router, try moving it closer or adding a Wi-Fi extender.

fixSteps.title: Re-pair in the app
fixSteps.instruction: In the Clean Home+ app, remove the device and re-add it following the pairing procedure.

fixSteps.title: Reset Wi-Fi on the purifier
fixSteps.instruction: Hold the Wi-Fi button on the purifier for 5 seconds to reset the wireless module. Then re-pair with the app.

whenToCallPro: If the Wi-Fi module has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Philips air purifier Wi-Fi connection Clean Home app fix

faq.q: Does the Philips purifier work without Wi-Fi?
faq.a: Yes, all core functions work with the physical controls. Wi-Fi adds app control and monitoring.

faq.q: Why does Philips need 2.4GHz?
faq.a: The Wi-Fi module only supports 2.4GHz. Ensure your router has this band enabled.

faq.q: What is the Clean Home+ app?
faq.a: The Philips Clean Home+ app provides remote control, air quality monitoring, and filter management.

title: Filter Not Detected
shortDescription: The purifier cannot detect an installed filter.
fullDescription: F0 means the filter detection system cannot confirm a filter is installed. The filter may not be properly seated, the filter cover not fully closed, or the NFC/RFID tag is not readable.

causes.cause: Filter not properly seated in its slot

fixSteps.title: Reseat the filter
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug. Open the filter panel and remove the filter. Reinsert it firmly, ensuring correct orientation (follow the arrows).

fixSteps.title: Close the cover properly
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the filter cover is fully closed and latched. Some models have a magnetic or mechanical latch.

fixSteps.title: Try a different filter
fixSteps.instruction: If available, try another genuine Philips filter to rule out a damaged RFID tag.

fixSteps.title: Clean the sensor area
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the area where the filter contacts the sensor with a dry cloth.

whenToCallPro: If the filter reader sensor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Philips air purifier F0 filter not detected fix

faq.q: Does Philips require genuine filters?
faq.a: Models with NFC/RFID detection require genuine Philips filters with proper tags.

faq.q: Can I use third-party filters?
faq.a: Third-party filters without NFC/RFID tags will trigger F0 on compatible models.

faq.q: Is F0 the same as a filter replacement alert?
faq.a: No. F0 means the filter is not detected at all. Filter replacement alerts (A3, C7, HEPA) mean the filter is detected but expired.

title: Humidity Sensor Malfunction (2-in-1 Models)
shortDescription: The humidity sensor is not reading correctly on purifier-humidifier combo models.
fullDescription: On Philips 2-in-1 air purifier and humidifier models, the humidity sensor error means the relative humidity readings are inaccurate or the sensor has failed, affecting the automatic humidification function.

causes.cause: Sensor covered with mineral dust from humidification

symptoms: Humidity reading stuck or showing dashes
Readings inconsistent with a separate hygrometer
Humidifier runs continuously despite high humidity

fixSteps.title: Clean the humidity sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the sensor opening and use a dry cotton swab to clean inside. Mineral dust from humidification can coat the sensor.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle for recalibration
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 60 seconds. The sensor will recalibrate when powered back on.

fixSteps.title: Run in a stable environment
fixSteps.instruction: Let the sensor calibrate for 30 minutes in a room with stable humidity away from direct moisture sources.

fixSteps.title: Use distilled water
fixSteps.instruction: Mineral deposits from tap water can affect the sensor. Switch to distilled water for humidification.

whenToCallPro: If the humidity sensor has permanently failed.
videoSearchQuery: Philips air purifier humidifier humidity sensor error fix

faq.q: Why does the humidity reading seem wrong?
faq.a: Mineral dust from humidification can coat the sensor. Clean it regularly with a dry cotton swab.

faq.q: Should I use distilled water?
faq.a: Yes, distilled or demineralized water produces less mineral dust and extends the life of the sensor and filters.

faq.q: How accurate is the built-in humidity sensor?
faq.a: Typically within +/- 5% RH under normal conditions.
